visual:
NEW data context: "Minutes of Practice" (VERTICAL orientation)
  - Vertical Data Table: Guitar: 27, Piano: 14, Violin: 41, Drums: 33
guide: "Here's data about how many minutes students practiced their instruments."
guide_2: "Check: are these numbers multiples of 5? Which scale is best for this data?"
prompt: "Which scale is best for this data?"
student_action: [Select: Scale of 1 / Scale of 2 / Scale of 5 / Scale of 10]
on_correct: "Scale of 2. You saw that 14, 27, 33, 41 aren't multiples of 5 so we need scale of 2 to graph them precisely. Scale of 2 can work when the largest value is 41."
on_incorrect: (5 or 10) "These numbers aren't multiples of 5 so the values can't be shown exactly with that scale. Try scale of 2."
on_incorrect_2: (1) "Scale of 1 lets us graph exact numbers, but the largest value is 41. That would be a lot of marks on the axis. Which scale would be exact and look cleaner?"
remediation: (Light) "Check those ones digits: 7, 4, 1, 3. None are 0 or 5. Which scale works for any number?"
visual_2:
same data context: "Minutes of Practice" (VERTICAL orientation bar graph)
  - Vertical Data Table: Guitar: 27, Piano: 14, Violin: 41, Drums: 33
visual_3: Bar Graph creation tool with Scale of 2 active. Gridlines visible at each tick mark (consistent with standard graph display).
student_action_2: Set bar heights for all 4 categories (click-to-set)
on_complete: Graph displays with all 4 bars correctly placed.
guide_4: "You chose the right scale and created the graph. Every bar lands exactly where it should."
